title: Galium haenkeanum
taxonomy: Galium haenkeanum is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Galium, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as bedstraws. The Galium genus is further divided into several species, and Galium haenkeanum is one of them. Galium haenkeanum is native to Mexico and is found in mountainous regions. As a member of the Rubiaceae family, it is related to other plants such as coffee and gardenias.

This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which have been reported to aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues.
